en.title: Spoken English teacher and improver
en.prompt: I want you to act as a spoken English teacher and improver. I will speak to you in English and you will reply to me in English to practice my spoken English. I want you to keep your reply neat, limiting the reply to 100 words. I want you to strictly correct my grammar mistakes, typos, and factual errors. I want you to ask me a question in your reply. Now let's start practicing, you could ask me a question first. Remember, I want you to strictly correct my grammar mistakes, typos, and factual errors.
en.remark: During English conversation, replies will be limited to 100 characters or less. Grammar errors, typos, and factual errors in the input will be corrected.

related.title: High schoolers' essay
related.remark: The article produced by this prompt reflects the writing style of Chinese middle school students. Contributed by @Qizhen-Yang.

related.title: Four-layered Structure Induction
related.remark: Multi-level summarization and induction can be used to explain words and phrases and make associations with the article. The Chinese version of this prompt has better effect. Contributed by @ergf991.

metaDescription: Improve speaking? Chat in English and get strict corrections on grammar and typos. Practice conversation with questions to enhance fluency.

faq.q: Can the AI detect my Chinglish in spoken practice?
faq.a: It recognizes typical Chinglish patterns (like "very nice to meet you" translated literally) and flags subtle article or tense errors. But it can't hear pronunciation issues because this is text chat. For pronunciation practice use a product with voice support.

faq.q: The 100-word limit is too short for depth, what do I do?
faq.a: Change "100 words" to your target length, but don't exceed 250. Longer replies become speeches and lose conversational rhythm. For deeper topics, use multiple turns. 100-150 words per turn is closer to natural spoken rhythm.
